9 Star Google Review Free Quotes NHK Tiling has been breathing new life into spaces one tile at a time since 1995. Based in Sydney, we’ve completed residential, commercial, and industrial tiling projects in and around the Hills District. Our team of fully licensed and qualified tiling experts can help you with interior as well as exterior tiling jobs. As a family-owned and operated business, relationships are the building blocks of our business.

Here’s the short and sweet version: More than 25 years of experience Loads of testimonials highlighting our workmanship Comprehensive insurance All tilers are licensed and qualified We have completed thousands of projects in the Hills District, Eastern Suburbs, and wider Sydney area. If you ask around, you’ll probably find that we’ve completed a tiling project for a friend, family, or colleague.

The final cost will depend on factors like the tiling material, area size, and difficulty of the project. If there’s a lot of prep work (like uneven flooring or old tiles that need to be fixed or removed first) or you use very small tiles, the installation will be more complex which will push up the cost. To give you an idea, the average labour cost for outdoor tiling is $45 per hour or $45 per m² while for pool tiling it’s almost double that.

4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Rock wall cladding is a non-structural layer that’s used to cover a bare wall’s surface. It can either be simulated or real stone, and there’s a huge range of different colors, types of stones, and textures that you can choose from. From your patio to an accent wall in your bathroom, there are many popular areas where you can install rock wall claddings. Not only is it decorative, but it can also be functional.

Ceramic floors ($50 to $60 per m²) are typically cheaper than porcelain floors ($60 to $90 per m²), while natural stone is usually the most expensive (starting at about $80 per m²).

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=Are tiles durable enough for Sydney’s climate and lifestyle. _builder_version=4. 27. 5 _module_preset=default hover_enabled=0 global_colors_info={} open=off sticky_enabled=0] Yes. Our selection of tiles are more than durable enough for Sydney’s climate and lifestyle when the right materials are chosen and installed properly.

With Sydney’s mix of heat, humidity, coastal air, heavy rain, and busy households, tiles are one of the most reliable surface options available — which is exactly why we recommend them so often. Here’s why they perform so well 👇 Built for Sydney’s weather: Quality tiles — particularly porcelain and properly sealed natural stone — handle temperature changes, moisture, and UV exposure without warping, swelling, or fading.

16 _module_preset=default custom_margin=0px||0px||false|false custom_padding=0px||0px||false|false collapsed=off global_colors_info={}][et_pb_row column_structure=1_2,1_2 use_custom_gutter=on gutter_width=2 make_equal=on _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default max_width=100% module_alignment=center custom_margin=0px||0px||false|false custom_padding=0px||0px||false|false collapsed=off global_colors_info={}][et_pb_column type=1_2 disabled_on=off|off|off _builder_version=4. 27.

Moisture & mould resistance Outdoor-ready options: For external areas, we specify tiles with appropriate slip ratings, low water absorption, and frost and UV resistance to ensure safety and longevity — even during wet weather or hot summers. Installation matters: Durability isn’t just about the tile — it’s about how it’s installed.

Tiles don’t absorb moisture like softer surfaces, which helps prevent mould, rot or swelling, and lingering odours Perfect for busy lifestyles: Tiles are tough and can cope with high foot traffic, spills & damage, busy foot traffic (kids and pets), all with minimal cleaning required. Unlike timber or some laminates, tiles don’t scratch easily and won’t be damaged by water.

9 Star Google Review Free Quotes Quite often, people tend to focus more on the interior of their Sydney home or office spaces while ignoring the exteriors. This is especially true of outdoor tiling. While the interior spaces convey to the visitors who you are, the exterior gives them the first impression about who you might be. Beautifying your garden is an excellent way to enhance the exterior decor.

However, your green space only assumes its fullest glory when theres a beautifully tiled path that leads your guests to your doorstep. However, exterior tiling is easier said than done. Primarily, this is because of the prolonged exposure to natural weathering agents such as air, water, wind and sunlight, among many others. To ensure that such factors are accounted for, at NHK tiling, we offer a range of different Exterior and Outdoor Tiling Services Sydney.

4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Outdoor tiling can be a stylish and novel addition to your home that increases the value when you sell it and enjoy it while you are in it. Tiles require less maintenance than wood, lasts longer, and can look far better if you choose the right materials. But it’s not an easy decision to make. There are many tiling materials, so many styles, and so many methods of installation and maintenance, which can result in decision paralysis.

The tiles need to be sealed so that they do not absorb moisture, which is more of a problem for outdoor tiles exposed directly to the elements. This also means that you can easily clean them with water and a tile-compatible cleaning agent. Of course, if you are using porcelain or ceramic, then you may not have to seal them at all, as they are naturally moisture resistant.

Natural Stone Tiles – Variants such as marble, travertine, and limestone bring elegance through unique textures and tones. They require sealing but offer timeless beauty. Mosaic Tiles – Small-scale pieces arranged into intricate designs. Perfect for feature walls, niches, or shower floors where detail matters. Terrazzo Tiles – Composite models blending marble, quartz, and glass chips within cement or resin. Known for longevity and a distinctive speckled look that adds artistic flair.

For homeowners seeking a luxurious feel, natural stone varieties like marble or travertine add sophistication but need periodic sealing to maintain their finish. Meanwhile, mosaic tiles excel in showers or feature walls, offering creative flexibility without overwhelming the space. [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=Do You Handle Waterproofing Before Tiling a Bathroom. _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Yes.

This makes it ideal for showers, floors, and splash zones that experience frequent water contact. While epoxy grout costs more and takes skill to apply, the payoff lies in long-term durability and reduced maintenance. It keeps joints looking clean and intact even after years of use. [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=How Do I Choose Non-Slip Tiles for Wet Areas. _builder_version=4. 27.

Once cured, the surface becomes the ideal base for flawless tile adhesion. [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=Should I Use Epoxy Grout in My Bathroom. _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Epoxy grout is an excellent choice for bathrooms, especially in areas exposed to constant moisture or heavy cleaning. Unlike traditional cement-based varieties, it forms a dense, non-porous seal that resists stains, mould, and discolouration.

Our professional tilers manage full waterproofing before any tile is laid. This critical stage safeguards walls and floors from moisture penetration, preventing leaks, mould growth, and long-term structural damage. Our licensed bathroom tiling team applies industry-approved membranes across all wet zones, including showers, baths, and splash areas. Corners, joints, and edges receive additional sealing for complete protection.

4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Keeping tiles spotless and free from mould starts with regular maintenance and good ventilation. After each shower, open windows or run an exhaust fan to reduce humidity — moisture left lingering encourages mildew growth. Use a gentle pH-neutral cleaner and a soft cloth weekly to remove soap film without dulling the finish. Avoid harsh chemicals or abrasive pads that can damage grout and tile surfaces.

For extra protection, apply a grout sealer annually to block water absorption and keep joints looking fresh. Epoxy grout or high-quality sealants also help prevent discolouration, while professional deep cleaning every year restores shine and hygiene. Consistent care keeps your bathroom bright, healthy, and easier to maintain long-term. [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=Do You Offer a Warranty or Guarantee on Bathroom Tiling Work. _builder_version=4. 27.

Textured finishes or matte glazes improve traction without sacrificing design appeal, while smaller formats like mosaics add extra grout lines that increase grip naturally. Materials such as porcelain and stone-look options provide both style and function when chosen with the correct surface treatment. [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=How Do I Keep Bathroom Tiles Clean and Mould-Free. _builder_version=4. 27.

4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Selecting non-slip tiles for bathrooms and other wet zones requires balancing safety, comfort, and aesthetics. Start by checking the slip rating, usually marked as an R-value (R9–R13). Higher ratings indicate a stronger grip — ideal for showers, floors, and entry points where surfaces often stay damp.

Before proceeding, the area must be thoroughly cleaned to remove soap residue and oils that could weaken adhesion. Professional tilers inspect the original layer carefully, applying a bonding primer or adhesive suitable for smooth substrates. This approach saves demolition time, reduces mess, and lowers labour costs. However, it’s not suitable if tiles are loose, uneven, or conceal moisture damage. [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=What Type of Tiles Are Best for Sydney Bathrooms.

_builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Sydney’s coastal humidity and temperature shifts call for tiles that resist moisture, heat, and wear. Porcelain tiles top the list for local bathrooms — their dense structure and low absorption rate handle steam and splashes effortlessly. Ceramic options remain popular for walls due to affordability and vast design selection, while textured finishes or matte surfaces improve slip resistance on floors.

4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] Ceramic and porcelain tiles are the most commonly used, and there is little separating the two. Both are affordable, durable, stylish, and easy to maintain. They also come in a variety of patterns and colours. Porcelain is a little better than ceramic tiles, however. This is because porcelain tiles are denser. Denser tiles are stronger and are also less porous, meaning they are resistant to water.

Once you do have tiles on the wall, all you have to do is clean them once in a while. And even the cleaning is far easier than other products such as wood or metal. While you could use paint, it does not have the same elegant appeal and is not as environmentally friendly. Tiles are easy to install compared to other materials, such as stone or marble. If you are tiling over existing tile, then you are dealing with an extra layer of thickness and you need to ensure the wall can support it. You should not tile over plywood, wallpaper, or glossy surfaces. Medium-density fibreboard (‘MDF’) or chipboard can also be tiled over, but not in wet or humid areas.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=What is the difference between floor and wall tiles. _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={} open=off] The primary difference between floor and wall tiles is that floor tiles have to be stronger as they will have increased usage and pressure. Also, wall tiles can be more difficult to install.

Floor tiles are automatically supported by the ground and pushed down by gravity. The floor is naturally going to be flat. But wall tiles need to be supported by intelligent design and the surface of the wall needs to be smooth and strong. Wall tile is thus thinner and slicker and is not recommended for floors. Privacy Policy What is a privacy policy. A privacy policy is a statement or a legal document that sets out some or all of the ways a business collects, uses, and manages a customer’s personal information. It is also known as a ‘responsible use of data’, or a ‘use of private and confidential information policy’. But for our purposes, let’s just stick to calling it a privacy policy because that’s a lot easier to remember. What is personal information.

Passive Information Collection We may also collect information which tells us about visitors to Our Website. jpg alt=painting title_text=painting _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}][/et_pb_image][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}] What to Consider When Choosing Between Tiling and Painting Efficiency Tiling projects often require longer preparation, precise cutting, and meticulous installation, yet once completed, tiled spaces rarely need follow-up work.

While its versatility is unmatched, painting also carries limitations that deserve consideration. Pros Quick Transformation Painting provides fast results, refreshing spaces in days rather than weeks. Budget-Friendly Generally cheaper, especially for larger surface areas, making it appealing for cost-conscious households. Wide Colour Range Thousands of shades are available, offering unmatched flexibility in personalising interiors.

Paint, on the other hand, often requires reapplication and carries environmental considerations linked to production, chemicals, and disposal. Maintenance Tile surfaces generally require little upkeep — routine cleaning often suffices. Painted areas need regular attention, especially in high-traffic or moisture-prone zones, with frequent touch-ups essential to maintain a polished appearance.

Simple Updates Repainting allows easy style changes without structural alterations. Cons Shorter Lifespan Paint tends to chip, peel, or fade more quickly, especially in humid environments like bathrooms. Ongoing Maintenance Walls require regular touch-ups to maintain a fresh, clean look. Vulnerable to Moisture Moisture-prone zones such as laundries and kitchens challenge paint durability. Stylish Versatility Available in ceramic, porcelain, natural stone, and mosaics, tiles adapt to modern, classic, or coastal interiors. Easy Maintenance Grout and surface cleaning is straightforward, particularly with high-quality finishes and epoxy options. Boosts Resale Appeal A tiled space often impresses buyers, creating a perception of luxury and durability.

Painting, in contrast, can transform rooms quickly, usually within days. However, paint’s shorter life cycle means reapplication will be needed more often, creating repeat disruptions over time. Tile Size and Layout Tile size plays a crucial role in the overall visual impact of your bathroom. Large-format tiles create a seamless, open feel by reducing the number of grout lines, making them an excellent choice for small bathrooms.

Matte Finish Choosing between a glossy or matte finish depends on both style and functionality. Glossy tiles reflect more light, making them ideal for small bathrooms that need a brighter and more open appearance. They’re also easy to wipe clean, making them a low-maintenance choice for busy households. Matte tiles, on the other hand, offer a softer, more natural look that hides water spots and smudges better than glossy tiles.

In high-humidity areas like bathrooms, it’s essential to choose tiles that can withstand constant exposure to water without deteriorating. Porcelain and ceramic tiles are among the most popular choices due to their non-porous nature, while marble and zellige tiles require sealing to prevent water damage. Another aspect to consider is resistance to stains, cracks, and scratches. While glossy ceramic tiles are easy to clean, they may be more prone to visible scratches than matte or textured options.

Narrow grout lines with rectified-edge tiles also help reduce cleaning efforts, keeping your bathroom looking pristine with minimal upkeep. Slip Resistance and Safety While wall tiles don’t typically need to be slip-resistant, some homeowners prefer to extend their wall tiles onto floors for a cohesive look. In these cases, it’s important to choose tiles with a matte or textured surface to prevent slips and falls, especially in wet areas like shower enclosures.

Maintenance and Cleaning Requirements Some white tiles require more upkeep than others, so it’s crucial to choose an option that aligns with your lifestyle. Glossy ceramic and porcelain tiles are easy to wipe clean, while textured tiles or intricate mosaics may require more effort to keep grout lines and surfaces spotless. If you prefer a low-maintenance option, look for tiles with stain-resistant coatings or minimal grout lines.

Porcelain and ceramic tiles are generally more budget-friendly, whereas marble, terrazzo, and handmade zellige tiles are premium options that require a higher investment. It’s important to factor in not only the cost of the tiles themselves but also installation, grout, sealing (for porous materials), and maintenance. If you’re working with a tighter budget, consider ceramic tiles that mimic the look of more expensive materials like marble or terrazzo without the high price tag.

For added safety, consider anti-slip coatings or textured finishes on wall tiles near the shower to prevent condensation from making them overly slick. Lighting and Room Size Impact Lighting can dramatically affect how white tiles look in your bathroom. Glossy tiles reflect both natural and artificial light, making a dimly lit bathroom feel brighter and more spacious. Matte or textured tiles absorb more light, creating a softer and more subdued atmosphere.

Natural stone tiles, while beautiful, require regular sealing to protect against water damage and discolouration. For easy maintenance, opt for porcelain tiles with a high water-resistance rating, especially in areas prone to splashes, such as the shower. If you’re set on stone tiles, be prepared for periodic sealing to keep them looking pristine. Budget – Finding the Right Balance Between Cost and Quality Tile prices can vary significantly based on material, size, and finish.

While high-end natural stone and designer tiles add luxury, they can be costly and require more maintenance. Fortunately, porcelain and ceramic tiles offer a more budget-friendly alternative while mimicking the look of premium materials like marble or terrazzo. When planning your budget, consider not just the tile cost but also installation, sealing (if needed), and long-term maintenance.

Slip Resistance – Safety First, Style Second Bathrooms can be slippery, especially when wet, so choosing tiles with the right slip resistance is essential—especially for the floor. Porcelain and ceramic tiles with a matte or textured finish provide better grip, reducing the risk of slips and falls. For added safety, consider smaller tiles with more grout lines, as the extra texture improves traction.

Natural stone tiles, while elegant, need regular sealing and careful cleaning to prevent water damage. If you love the stone look but not the maintenance, opt for stone-look porcelain tiles instead. Climate Considerations – Adapting to Sydney’s Conditions Sydney’s climate, with its humidity and temperature fluctuations, can impact tile performance over time. Porcelain tiles are a great choice for withstanding these conditions, as they are moisture-resistant and durable.

Sometimes, investing a little more in durable, high-quality tiles can save money on repairs and replacements in the long run. Maintenance – Keeping Your Bathroom Looking Fresh Some tiles require more upkeep than others. For low-maintenance bathrooms, choose stain-resistant, easy-to-clean materials like porcelain and glazed ceramic. These tiles resist mould, mildew, and grime, making them perfect for busy households.

Glossy tiles reflect light, creating a sense of openness and brightness—perfect for walls and feature areas. They are also easy to clean, making them a practical choice for splash zones. Matte tiles, on the other hand, offer a more subtle, contemporary look and are excellent for flooring since they provide better slip resistance. If you want the best of both worlds, consider using matte tiles on the floor for safety and glossy tiles on walls for brightness.

For example, white grout with white tiles eliminates harsh lines, enhancing openness. Conversely, contrasting grout (such as black grout with white tiles) highlights individual tiles, which can create a busier look. While this can be stylish, it’s best used in moderation for accent walls or splashbacks rather than throughout the entire bathroom.

Tile Pattern and Layout – Enhancing the Perception of Space The way tiles are arranged can significantly impact the overall look of your bathroom. Vertical stacking, for example, can make a ceiling appear higher, while a herringbone or chevron pattern can create a sense of movement and elongation. Diagonal tile layouts are another clever trick, making floors seem wider than they actually are. For small bathrooms, simplicity is key. Avoid overly intricate patterns that can make the space feel busy.

If you love glossy tiles, use them for walls and stick to slip-resistant options for flooring to maintain both safety and style. Water and Stain Resistance – Choosing the Right Material Since bathrooms are exposed to constant moisture, selecting water-resistant tiles is crucial. Porcelain and glazed ceramic tiles are the best choices as they are non-porous, preventing water absorption and stains.

Instead, opt for a layout that enhances the room’s natural dimensions, such as stacked subway tiles, large horizontal planks, or continuous floor tiles that extend into the shower area. Grout Colour – A Small Detail That Makes a Big Difference Grout may seem like an afterthought, but its colour and thickness can impact how spacious your bathroom feels. Matching the grout colour with your tiles creates a seamless, uniform look, making the space feel larger.

Every bathroom is unique, and the best tile choices depend on your space, personal style, and practical needs. Using the right bathroom tile is a tried and tested strategy to create the impression of size and turn a small bathroom into a welcoming space that appears bigger than it is. At the same time, you need to know which design styles and strategies to use whether you’re installing a handful of new tiles or renovating your entire bathroom. The key to getting the most from your available space comes down to understanding which advice to follow and which to ignore.

Picking your favourite wall tiles, then going back to the drawing board to choose a floor tile colour is the fast track to unsightly clashes and frustrating finishes. When you build your bathroom’s colour palette around a holistic vision, it’s easier to add pops of colour in the form of faucets, shower heads, door handles and other fittings without distracting from your room.

This is because light and bright floors reflect light and help your bathroom appear more open and airy. Better still, this effect is improved if your bathroom enjoys natural sunlight so if you’re trying to make your space feel bigger, you can’t go wrong with lighter tones (e. g. white, grey, beige, cream, ivory and light taupe).

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_image src=https://www. nhktiling. com. au/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/What-tiling-colours-are-the-best-for-small-bathrooms_-02. jpg title_text=What tiling colours are the best for small bathrooms_ 02 _builder_version=4. 18. 0 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}][/et_pb_image][et_pb_text admin_label=Does that mean darker tiles are a bad idea. _builder_version=4. 18. 0 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}] Does that mean darker tiles are a bad idea.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}] Not at all. Although lighter tile colours are well known and widely utilised to create the illusion of space, it’s 100% possible to lay darker coloured tiles to open up a small bathroom space. And if the thought of an entirely dark floor or wall is too much, you can use dark tiles to add a sense of depth to your room without overwhelming the eye.

For example, laying different shades of white in your floor and wall tiles then elevate your space with timber and natural stone tones in your vanity and benchtop for a chic coastal feel. With design tips like this, it’s entirely possible to reduce visual clutter (since this is the #1 thing that makes small bathrooms look even smaller) and still make bold colour and design statements. DID YOU KNOW: Neutral colours like white, black and grey are known as ‘achromatic’.

Whether you opt for traditional white, a contemporary cream colour, a cool grey, or another light shade entirely, the neutral palette will help open up your space and present the illusion of space. NHK Tiling Tip: Interested in the advantages of light tones but not in love with white bathroom tiles. Try experimenting with your favourite colour finished as metallic or glossy tiles which reflect light and create an expanded sense of proportions without sacrificing your own personal style.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text admin_label=Can tile colour really help my bathroom look bigger. _builder_version=4. 18. 0 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}] Can tile colour really help my bathroom look bigger. [/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 27. 4 _module_preset=default global_colors_info={}] Absolutely. It’s a well-known design rule that choosing lighter coloured bathroom wall tiles and floor tiles can help a small floorspace appear bigger.

For example, tiling around the edge of your room can help your space look wider by drawing the eyeline outwards. While it’s true that light colours help expand a compact space, creatively placed darker tiles have the double effect of adding a touch of luxury while adding a sense of depth to walls or bathroom features. Remember, the colours you choose should be part of a wider design plan.

Here are some of the most popular tile colours dominating the design world this year and what each colour symbolises: Olive Green: Peace, harmony and sophistication Soft Blue: Calmness, stability and serenity Vibrant Orange: Happiness, creativity and optimism Classic White: Cleanliness and purity Black & Grey: Elegance, balance and enlightenment Got a favourite in mind. Keep reading to discover how to tap into the illusion of space.

You might be drawn to the simple, soft appearance of lighter colours. Or, you might enjoy the elegant and eye-catching finish that only darker tiles can bring. Whatever your preference, when it comes to settling on a colour, there are no shortages to choose from (and sometimes that’s the issue. ).
